up.method: qualitative growth response (OD600); no quantitative rate reported
qualitative; required for growth, no quantitative rate reported

sec.method: GC with flame-ionization detector; only qualitative (methane formation confirmed, not quantified as a rate)

growth_records.cite: Pradel N et al. Description and genome analysis of a novel archaeon isolated from a syntrophic pyrite-forming enrichment culture and reclassification of Methanospirillum hungatei strains GP1 and SK as Methanospirillum purgamenti sp. nov.. PLOS ONE (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0308405. [PMC11346949]
growth_records.snippet: The temperature range of strain J.3.6.1-F.2.7.3T was determined to be 25-40 C with an optimum at 35 C ... optimum at pH 7.0-7.2 and at a salinity of 0-15 g/l NaCl (optimum 3 g/l NaCl). Under optimal conditions, a maximum growth rate of 0.03
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaeon), pure culture. Measured experimental values: max growth rate mu=0.03 h-1 and generation (doubling) time 10 h under optimal conditions (35-37C, pH 7.0-7.2, 3 g/l NaCl, H2/CO2). Species-description text also gives generation time at 37C as 10.0-13.0 h and Table 3 lists 'Minimal doubling time 10.0-13.0 h' for this strain. INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY: reported mu=0.03 h-1 implies td=ln2/0.03=23.1 h, which conflicts with the reported td of 10 h (td=10 h implies mu=ln2/10=0.069 h-1). Both are reported verbatim; not reconciled here. Growth by OD600. Electron donors experimentally usable: H2, formate, and 2-propanol (2-propanol only with acetate, after ~3-week lag). No quantitative substrate-uptake or methane-secretion RATES (mmol/gDW/h) are reported, so uptake/secretion rate values left null (qualitative only). Ammonium (5-25 mM NH4Cl) caused no significant inhibition of growth or methane production. N2 usable as N source. Temperature range 20-45C (species-level statement); strain-specific 25-40C, optimum 35C.
